# Socio-Technical Risk Analyzer

## Reason

Many software risks are organizational risks expressed through code and workflow. This skill identifies systemic fragility without scoring or ranking individuals.

## Functionality

- Analyzes collaboration, ownership, governance, documentation, and AI workflow signals.
- Detects knowledge silos, low bus-factor areas, opaque workflows, and governance bypass.
- Estimates severity, confidence, and systemic consequences.
- Recommends non-punitive mitigations.

## Proper Use Cases

Use before major changes, during governance health checks, when onboarding is hard, or when critical areas lack visible ownership and rationale.

Do not use for surveillance, productivity scoring, or developer ranking.

## Best Practices

- Frame findings as system resilience issues.
- Avoid inferring intent from sparse evidence.
- Prefer documentation, review, ownership, and governance improvements over blame.
- Use Git/PR history only as contextual evidence.
